title: Service Outcomes, Node 24 LTS, and the TypeScript Toolchain · Contract Lens: Clean Build Changed Environment
summary: A community-service API counts routes as value, floats Node and npm versions, confuses TypeScript type erasure with validation, and cannot reproduce its build or startup decision. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts clean build changed environment increment in outcome, runtime, compiler, and reproducibility charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove a clean npm ci, type gate, test, build, artifact, and startup under a changed environment without warm local residue.
Use Node type stripping only for erasable syntax while keeping type checking, path transformation, module resolution, and production emission explicit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Clean Build Changed Environment · Theory Clean Build Changed Enviro…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2447 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay pressure. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a reproducible Node and TypeScript artifact instead of trusting local residue; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove a clean npm ci, type gate, test, build, artifact, and startup under a changed environment without warm local residue.
